example.english: The company will release the app tomorrow.
example.chinese: 公司明天會發布這個應用程式。

corrections.english: Level of excellence.
corrections.reason: The noun means level of excellence; use Traditional Chinese wording.

example.english: The quality of the fabric is high.
example.chinese: 這種布料的品質很高。

corrections.english: Sometime; at some unspecified time in the future.
corrections.reason: Someday means at an unspecified future time, not next time.

example.english: Someday I will visit Kyoto.
example.chinese: 有一天我會去京都。

corrections.english: The medium of radio communication.
corrections.reason: The noun means the medium of radio communication.

example.english: The message was sent by wireless.
example.chinese: 訊息是透過無線電發出的。

corrections.english: To stir or mix (a salad).
corrections.reason: In cooking, toss means to stir or mix, especially a salad.

example.english: Toss the salad with olive oil.
example.chinese: 用橄欖油拌沙律。

corrections.english: Being of advantage, beneficial.
corrections.reason: The adjective means beneficial, not the noun “advantage”.

example.english: The new policy is advantageous to small businesses.
example.chinese: 新政策對小企業是有利的。

corrections.english: (football, hockey, basketball) The playing of the ball between the legs of an opponent.
corrections.reason: This sports sense means playing the ball between an opponent’s legs.

example.english: The striker scored with a nutmeg.
example.chinese: 前鋒用穿襠動作得分。

corrections.english: (UK, law) A police officer or an officer with equivalent powers.
corrections.reason: In UK law, a constable is a police officer, not a military policeman.

example.english: The constable asked for his name.
example.chinese: 警員詢問了他的姓名。

corrections.english: An office holder, a person holding an official position in government, sports, or other organization.
corrections.reason: The noun means a person holding an official position, not the adjective official.

example.english: A government official visited the school.
example.chinese: 一名政府官員到訪學校。

corrections.english: Of or pertaining to the practice of medicine.
corrections.reason: The adjective means relating to medicine, not a doctor.

example.english: She needs medical treatment.
example.chinese: 她需要醫療的治療。

corrections.english: A pilot of glider aircraft.
corrections.reason: This noun sense is a pilot of glider aircraft, not the aircraft itself.

example.english: The glider waited for better wind.
example.chinese: 那名滑翔機飛行員等待更好的風勢。

corrections.english: One of the title characters of Shakespeare's play Romeo and Juliet.
corrections.reason: The Shakespeare character is Juliet, not the moon Juliet.

example.english: Juliet is one of Shakespeare’s famous characters.
example.chinese: 茱麗葉是莎士比亞著名角色之一。

corrections.english: (star) A bright yellow-orange star in the constellation Boötes; Alpha (α) Boötis. It is the fourth-brightest star in the night sky.
corrections.reason: Arcturus is the bright star Alpha Boötis.

example.english: Arcturus is visible in the night sky.
example.chinese: 大角星在夜空中可見。

corrections.english: A country in Central Asia. Official name: Republic of Uzbekistan. Capital: Tashkent.
corrections.reason: The entry denotes the country, not only the ethnonym root.

example.english: Uzbekistan is in Central Asia.
example.chinese: 烏茲別克斯坦位於中亞。

corrections.english: To surprise greatly.
corrections.reason: Astonish means to surprise greatly, not “pleasant surprise”.

example.english: The news astonished everyone.
example.chinese: 這消息使大為驚訝的觀眾安靜下來。

corrections.english: (of a person) cultured, refined, educated
corrections.reason: Of a person, cultivated means cultured or refined, not farmed.

example.english: He is a cultivated speaker.
example.chinese: 他是一位有教養的演說者。

corrections.english: (grammar) The conditional mood.
corrections.reason: The grammar noun is the conditional mood, not the adjective “conditional”.

example.english: The conditional is common in grammar lessons.
example.chinese: 條件式常見於文法課。

corrections.english: Of or relating to linguistics.
corrections.reason: The adjective means relating to linguistics.

example.english: The article discusses linguistic change.
example.chinese: 這篇文章討論語言學的變化。

corrections.english: Relying upon; depending upon.
corrections.reason: The adjective means relying upon someone or something.

example.english: The project is dependent on funding.
example.chinese: 這個項目是依賴的資金支持。

corrections.english: The dangling mass of a pendulum or plumb line.
corrections.reason: The noun is the dangling mass of a pendulum or plumb line.

example.english: The bob hung at the end of the string.
example.chinese: 鉛錘掛在線的末端。

corrections.english: A en given name.
corrections.reason: The given-name entry should be Samuel, not the Book of Samuel.

example.english: Samuel signed the letter.
example.chinese: 塞繆爾簽了那封信。

corrections.english: (astronomy) The passage of a celestial body or other object across the observer's meridian, or across the disk of a larger celestial body.
corrections.reason: The astronomy sense includes passage across a meridian or across a larger disk.

example.english: The transit was recorded by the observatory.
example.chinese: 天文台記錄了這次中天現象。

corrections.english: A sliding pin or bar in a lock or latch mechanism.
corrections.reason: A bolt is a sliding pin or bar in a lock or latch.

example.english: Slide the bolt to lock the door.
example.chinese: 滑動門閂把門鎖上。

corrections.english: A heuristic method. [from 1860]
corrections.reason: Convert simplified/variant Chinese to standard Traditional Chinese for the heuristic-method sense.

corrections.english: A crown of laurel.
corrections.reason: This sense is a crown of laurel, not a garland in general.

corrections.english: The capital city of the province of Quebec, Canada.
corrections.reason: The English definition is the capital city of Quebec, not the province in general.

corrections.english: (US) The United States Senate.
corrections.reason: The English definition is specifically the United States Senate.

corrections.new_chinese: 美國國家航空暨太空總署
corrections.english: Acronym of National Aeronautics and Space Administration.
corrections.reason: NASA expands to the United States National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

corrections.english: Acronym of w:Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries.
corrections.reason: OPEC is the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries.

corrections.english: Acronym of North Atlantic Treaty Organization
corrections.reason: NATO is the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.

corrections.english: A slipper lobster (a crustacean of the family Scyllaridae).
corrections.reason: The English definition is slipper lobster, not lobster in general.

corrections.english: A state of Mexico.
corrections.reason: The English definition is a state of Mexico.

corrections.english: Influenza.
corrections.reason: Influenza is commonly and accurately rendered as 流感.

corrections.new_chinese: 電子郵件地址；電郵地址
corrections.english: (countable, informal) An email address.
corrections.reason: The countable informal sense is an email address, not an email message.

corrections.english: (intransitive) To grow teeth.
corrections.reason: To teethe means to grow teeth.

corrections.english: The floor or bottom of the sea or ocean.
corrections.reason: The seabed is the floor or bottom of the sea or ocean.

corrections.english: The capital city of Kyrgyzstan.
corrections.reason: Convert the capital-city name Bishkek to standard Traditional Chinese.

corrections.english: Fair, just, or impartial.
corrections.reason: Equitable means fair, just, or impartial.

corrections.english: Of or relating to an epic.
corrections.reason: The adjective means of or relating to an epic.

corrections.english: A meat or fish jelly.
corrections.reason: Convert simplified 肉冻 to Traditional Chinese.

corrections.english: A district of the city of Antwerp, province of Antwerp, Belgium.
corrections.reason: Convert simplified/variant Antwerp transliteration to Traditional Chinese.

corrections.english: A village in Vasylkivka settlement hromada, Synelnykove Raion, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, Ukraine.
corrections.reason: Use the Traditional Chinese transliteration for Debaltseve.

corrections.old_chinese: 博伊基夫斯凱鎮級市鎮
corrections.english: A rural settlement, the administrative centre of Boikivske settlement hromada, Kalmiuske Raion, Donetsk Oblast, Ukraine, founded by separatist Evangelical Lutherans from the Swabian colony of Neuhoffnung (now the western part of Osypenko) in 1869; in the wake of the Second World War, its German population was expelled and it was repopulated by Boiky forcibly relocated from Czarna Górna (now Czarna); controlled by the Donetsk People’s Republic since August 2014.
corrections.reason: Use the settlement name rather than the wider administrative hromada label.

corrections.english: The capital city of Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan.
corrections.reason: The English definition is the capital city of Fukuoka Prefecture.

corrections.english: A county-level city of Nantong, Jiangsu, China.
corrections.reason: The English definition is a county-level city of Nantong, Jiangsu.

corrections.english: A prefecture-level city of Jilin, China.
corrections.reason: The English definition is a prefecture-level city of Jilin, China.

corrections.english: The largest island in the Maltese Archipelago.
corrections.reason: The English definition is the largest island in the Maltese Archipelago.

corrections.english: (computing) An input device that is moved over a pad or other flat surface to produce a corresponding movement of a pointer on a graphical display.
corrections.reason: The computing sense is an input device, not the animal.

corrections.english: (fused relative) The place in, at or to which.
corrections.reason: Convert simplified 哪里 to Traditional Chinese for the fused-relative where sense.

corrections.english: From the time that.
corrections.reason: Since as a conjunction means from the time that.

corrections.english: A male child. [from 15th c.]
corrections.reason: The English definition is a male child; 男僕 is the wrong sense.

example.english: Jack is the most intelligent boy in the class.
example.chinese: 傑克是班里最聰明的男孩兒 。

corrections.english: A greeting used when answering the telephone.
corrections.reason: The telephone greeting sense can be 喂 or 你好.

corrections.english: A male person, usually an adult; a (generally adult male) sentient being, whether human, supernatural, elf, alien, etc.
corrections.reason: This sense is an adult male person, not person in general.

corrections.english: Of a somewhat high temperature, often but not always connoting that the high temperature is pleasant rather than uncomfortable.
corrections.reason: Warm is pleasantly somewhat hot, not simply 熱.

corrections.english: An evildoer.
corrections.reason: Convert simplified 坏人 and clarify malefactor as an evildoer.

corrections.english: Very; extremely.
corrections.reason: Terribly in this sense means very/extremely, not frightening.

corrections.english: Bright and pleasant.
corrections.reason: Cheerful here means bright and pleasant.

corrections.english: Naked, uncovered.
corrections.reason: Bare means naked or uncovered.

corrections.english: Tired; weary.
corrections.reason: Fatigued means tired or weary.

corrections.english: Light rain.
corrections.reason: Drizzle is light rain.

corrections.english: Appropriate; suitable.
corrections.reason: Fitting means appropriate or suitable.

corrections.english: Official.
corrections.reason: Formal in this sense means official/formal.

corrections.english: To make a groan.
corrections.reason: To groan is to make a groan, not to rebuke.

corrections.english: Exactly, precisely, perfectly.
corrections.reason: Just in this adverbial sense means exactly or precisely.

corrections.english: To act as a referee.
corrections.reason: To referee means to act as a referee.

corrections.english: A pleasing combination of elements, or arrangement of sounds.
corrections.reason: Harmony in this sense is a pleasing combination or arrangement, broader than musical harmony.

corrections.english: A young person.
corrections.reason: A youngster is a young person.

corrections.english: (transitive, cooking) To cook with steam.
corrections.reason: To steam in cooking means to cook with steam; use a full Traditional Chinese definition rather than a one-character gloss.

corrections.english: (anatomy) The back part of the neck.
corrections.reason: The nape is the back part of the neck.

corrections.english: To have a tussle.
corrections.reason: To have a tussle means to struggle, scuffle, or fight; 吵架 is too narrow and only verbal.

corrections.english: Someone with an Aries star sign.
corrections.reason: This noun sense is someone with the Aries star sign, not the constellation/sign itself.

corrections.new_chinese: 奧林匹克運動會；奧運會
corrections.english: The Olympic Games.
corrections.reason: The Olympics means the Olympic Games.

example.english: He's watching the Olympics.
example.chinese: 他在看奧運會。

corrections.english: A prefecture of Japan.
corrections.reason: The English definition is a prefecture of Japan, not Kyoto city in general.

corrections.english: A tote bag.
corrections.reason: A tote in this sense is a tote bag, not a pocket.

corrections.english: (Roman god) The Roman god of war.
corrections.reason: This proper-noun sense is the Roman god Mars.

corrections.english: (transitive) to set fire to (something), to light (something)
corrections.reason: To ignite means to set fire to or light something.

corrections.english: (transitive) To have the use or benefit of something.
corrections.reason: This transitive sense means to have the use or benefit of something.

corrections.english: The "Enter" key on a computer keyboard.
corrections.reason: The noun sense is the Enter key on a keyboard.

corrections.english: While online; while in a running or active state, or connected to the Internet.
corrections.reason: Online as an adverb means while connected to the Internet or active system.

corrections.english: To make a short reference to something.
corrections.reason: To mention means to make a short reference to something.

corrections.english: (colloquial, also, attributively) Ellipsis of giant panda (“Ailuropoda melanoleuca”). [from 19th c.]
corrections.reason: This colloquial ellipsis refers specifically to the giant panda.

corrections.english: Not the same; exhibiting a difference.
corrections.reason: The adjective means not the same or exhibiting a difference.

example.english: Management of a company is different from ownership of a company.
example.chinese: 管理一家公司和擁有一家公司是捷然不同的。

corrections.english: Made of gold.
corrections.reason: The adjective means made of gold; 金 alone is too terse.

corrections.english: The capital city of Osaka Prefecture, Japan.
corrections.reason: The English definition is the capital city of Osaka Prefecture.

corrections.english: Confinement in prison.
corrections.reason: This noun sense is confinement in prison, not the building alone.

corrections.new_chinese: 巴爾幹的；巴爾幹半島的
corrections.english: Of or relating to the Balkan Peninsula.
corrections.reason: The adjective means of or relating to the Balkan Peninsula.

corrections.english: Related to a seaside.
corrections.reason: This adjective means related to a seaside.

corrections.english: The common people of a nation.
corrections.reason: Populace means the common people of a nation.

corrections.english: (modal) Actually; in fact; in reality.
corrections.reason: Really as a modal adverb means actually or in fact, not merely true.

corrections.english: A lawyer, as in King's Counsel (KC) or Queen's Counsel (QC).
corrections.reason: Counsel in this sense is a lawyer, especially as in King’s/Queen’s Counsel.

corrections.english: Something resembling a rose flower.
corrections.reason: This sense means something resembling a rose flower, not the flower itself.

corrections.english: A county-level city of Kashgar prefecture, Xinjiang autonomous region, China.
corrections.reason: The English definition is a county-level city of Kashgar prefecture.

corrections.english: A prairie province in western Canada. Capital: Regina.
corrections.reason: The English definition is a Canadian prairie province.

corrections.english: A lightning spark, i.e., a lightning bolt.
corrections.reason: A bolt in this sense is a lightning bolt.

corrections.english: (pejorative, of a person) Good-for-nothing; not dependable.
corrections.reason: The pejorative person sense means good-for-nothing or not dependable.

corrections.english: (transitive) To cause (something) to cease moving or progressing.
corrections.reason: To stop means to cause something to cease moving or progressing.

corrections.english: To ritualistically inter in a grave or tomb.
corrections.reason: To bury in this sense means to inter in a grave or tomb.

corrections.english: The (thing) here (used in indicating something or someone nearby).
corrections.reason: This determiner indicates a nearby thing or person.

corrections.english: recently made, created, discovered, or obtained
corrections.reason: The adjective means recently made, created, discovered, or obtained.

corrections.english: Of a living being, having lived for most of the expected years.
corrections.reason: This adjective describes a living being that has lived for most of the expected years.

corrections.english: In, at or to the place (that) or a place (that).
corrections.reason: Where means in, at, or to a place.

example.english: Where are you?
example.chinese: 你在哪兒?

corrections.english: (transitive) To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
corrections.reason: To see means to perceive with the eyes.

example.english: I'm glad to see you back.
example.chinese: 很高興再見到您。

corrections.english: (UK, AU, HK) A room, enclosed area or single-purpose building containing a fixture or fixtures used for urination and defecation; a bathroom or water closet. [from 19th c.]
corrections.reason: The room/building sense is a toilet or washroom.

example.english: Does it have a toilet?
example.chinese: 有廁所嗎？

corrections.english: A gemstone made from this mineral.
corrections.reason: The gemstone made from diamond mineral is 鑽石/金剛石.

example.english: The diamond was set in a gold ring.
example.chinese: 鑽石被镶在一個金戒指上。

corrections.english: An abstract entity used to describe quantity.
corrections.reason: A number is an abstract entity used to describe quantity.

example.english: Due to a number of unfortunate causes, one-parent families are on the increase.
example.chinese: 由於諸多不幸因素，單親家庭的數量在增長中。

corrections.english: A time period of indeterminate length, generally more than one year.
corrections.reason: Era means a time period, often of indeterminate length.

example.english: The revolution ushered in a new era.
example.chinese: 革命迎來了新時代。

corrections.english: a part of an aircraft that provides lift
corrections.reason: The aircraft-lift sense is specifically a wing of an aircraft.

example.english: Ice had formed on the aircraft's wings.
example.chinese: 飛機的機翼上結了冰。

corrections.english: not skilled or successful at doing something
corrections.reason: Bad at doing something means not skilled or successful at it.

corrections.english: moving or happening quickly
corrections.reason: Fast means moving or happening quickly.

example.english: This is a fast train.
example.chinese: 這是一列快速的火車。

corrections.english: (countable, physical chemistry) A chemical element or compound in such a state.
corrections.reason: This chemistry countable noun sense is a gas.

example.english: The gas gives off a strong, unpleasant smell.
example.chinese: 這個氣體散發出強烈難聞的氣味。

corrections.english: A barren area of land or desolate terrain, especially one with little water or vegetation; a wasteland.
corrections.reason: A desert is a barren area of land with little water or vegetation.

example.english: One third of the earth's surface is desert.
example.chinese: 地球表面的三分之一是沙漠。

corrections.english: real or genuine
corrections.reason: True in this adjective sense means real or genuine; keep 真正的 and add 真的 instead of narrowing to one term.

example.english: She is a true friend.
example.chinese: 她是真正的朋友。

corrections.english: to become a member of a group
corrections.reason: To join means to become a member of a group; 入伙 is too colloquial/narrow.

example.english: She joined the club.
example.chinese: 她加入了俱樂部。

corrections.english: A light meal.
corrections.reason: A snack is a light meal or small amount of food between meals.

corrections.english: Joy; pleasure.
corrections.reason: Delight means joy or pleasure.

corrections.english: to give birth to or produce
corrections.reason: To bear means to give birth to or produce; keep both birth and produce/fruit senses.

example.english: The tree bears fruit in summer.
example.chinese: 這棵樹在夏天結出果實。

corrections.english: to write or include items in a list
corrections.reason: To list means to write or include items in a list.

example.english: The guide lists several local restaurants.
example.chinese: 指南列出了幾間本地餐廳。

corrections.english: a business or company
corrections.reason: Firm in this noun sense means a business or company; keep the existing natural translation.

corrections.english: a poker hand in which all the cards are of the same suit
corrections.reason: A flush is a poker hand in which all cards are of the same suit.

example.english: She won the game with a flush.
example.chinese: 她以同花贏了這局牌。

corrections.english: Clothing.
corrections.reason: Apparel means clothing; 穿戴 is not the best noun gloss.

corrections.english: An error.
corrections.reason: Mistake means an error; keep the clear modern gloss.

example.english: You made the same mistake as last time.
example.chinese: 你犯了跟上次相同的錯誤。

corrections.english: (anatomy) Either corner of the eye, where the eyelids meet.
corrections.reason: Canthus is either corner of the eye.

corrections.english: to help someone
corrections.reason: To aid means to help someone.

example.english: The money will aid poor families.
example.chinese: 這筆錢會援助貧困家庭。

corrections.english: loyal and faithful
corrections.reason: True in the loyal sense means loyal and faithful.

corrections.english: to shoot a gun or weapon
corrections.reason: To fire a weapon means to shoot or discharge it.

example.english: The soldiers fired at the target.
example.chinese: 士兵向目標開火。

corrections.english: to have snow falling from the sky
corrections.reason: To snow means to have snow falling from the sky; 漫天飛舞 is too poetic.

example.english: It snowed all night.
example.chinese: 整晚都在下雪。

corrections.english: to discuss something
corrections.reason: To talk about something means to discuss it.

example.english: They talked about the plan.
example.chinese: 他們討論了這個計畫。

corrections.english: Foam.
corrections.reason: Froth means foam.

corrections.english: cloth used for making clothes or other items
corrections.reason: Material in this sense is cloth used for making clothes or other items.

example.english: We chose a soft material for the curtains.
example.chinese: 我們為窗簾選了一種柔軟布料。

corrections.english: to put something in a particular position
corrections.reason: To place means to put something in a particular position.

corrections.english: to help someone do something
corrections.reason: To assist means to help someone do something.

example.english: A member of staff will assist you.
example.chinese: 一名職員會協助你。

corrections.english: At this moment, at present, now.
corrections.reason: Currently means at this moment, at present, now.

corrections.english: to receive, obtain, or buy something
corrections.reason: To get means to receive, obtain, or buy something.

example.english: I need to get a new phone.
example.chinese: 我需要得到一部新手機。

corrections.english: (by extension) The world of commerce and trade.
corrections.reason: Marketplace by extension means the world of commerce and trade.

corrections.english: physical strength or power
corrections.reason: Force in this noun sense means physical strength or power.

example.english: The door opened with force.
example.chinese: 門被用力量推開。

corrections.english: to go or come back, or give something back
corrections.reason: To return means to go or come back, or give something back.

example.english: Please return the book tomorrow.
example.chinese: 請明天歸還這本書。

corrections.english: to make an effort to do something
corrections.reason: To try means to make an effort to do something.

example.english: Try to open the door.
example.chinese: 嘗試打開門。

corrections.english: (transitive) To butcher animals, generally for food.
corrections.reason: To slaughter animals means to butcher them for food.

corrections.english: making little or no noise
corrections.reason: Quiet means making little or no noise; reject the noisy 悄悄 candidate.

corrections.english: To have respect for.
corrections.reason: To respect means to have respect for.

corrections.english: to fall as rain
corrections.reason: To rain means to fall as rain.

example.english: It may rain tonight.
example.chinese: 今晚可能會下雨。

corrections.english: A strip of fabric worn around the head.
corrections.reason: A headband is a strip of fabric worn around the head.

corrections.english: At the present time.
corrections.reason: Now means at the present time.

corrections.english: On the day before today.
corrections.reason: Yesterday means on the day before today.

corrections.english: frozen water
corrections.reason: Ice is frozen water.

example.english: The lake was covered with ice.
example.chinese: 湖面覆蓋著冰。

corrections.english: An amount abated; that which is taken away by way of reduction; deduction; decrease; a rebate or discount allowed; in particular from a tax. [Late 15th century.]
corrections.reason: Abatement is a reduction, deduction, decrease, or rebate; 推理 means inference and is unrelated.

example.english: The company applied for a tax abatement.
example.chinese: 那家公司申請稅項減免。

corrections.english: The quality or state of being able; capacity to do or of doing something; having the necessary power. [First attested from around (1350 to 1470).]
corrections.reason: Ability means capacity or power to do something; 人才 means talented person.

example.english: She has the ability to solve difficult problems.
example.chinese: 她有解決難題的能力。

corrections.new_chinese: 暫停；中止；所有權未定狀態
corrections.english: (legal) Expectancy; a condition when an ownership of real property is undetermined; lapse in succession of ownership of estate, or title. [Late 16th century]
corrections.reason: Abeyance means suspension or a legal state of undetermined ownership, not vacation.

example.english: The matter was held in abeyance until the court ruled.
example.chinese: 此事暫停處理，直到法院作出裁決。

corrections.english: (carboxylic acid) A yellowish resin acid isolated from rosin and used in varnishes and esters for plasticizers; C19H29COOH.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese 樅酸 and add common name 松香酸 for abietic acid.

example.english: Abietic acid is found in rosin.
example.chinese: 樅酸存在於松香之中。

corrections.english: The act of washing or cleansing the body, or some part of it, as a religious rite. [From mid 16th century.]
corrections.reason: Convert Simplified 仪式 to Traditional 儀式 and clarify the religious washing sense.

example.english: Ablution is performed before the prayer.
example.chinese: 祈禱前會進行淨身沐浴儀式。

corrections.english: (military, aeronautics) An early termination of a mission, action, or procedure in relation to missiles or spacecraft; the craft making such a mission.
corrections.reason: This noun sense is early termination of a mission or procedure, not abortion.

example.english: The launch ended in an abort.
example.chinese: 這次發射以任務中止告終。

corrections.english: (now, rare) The product of a miscarriage; an aborted offspring; an abortion. [First attested in the early 17th century.]
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese 墮胎 and include miscarriage sense 流產.

example.english: The old medical text referred to an abort.
example.chinese: 那本舊醫學文本提到流產或墮胎。

corrections.english: (obsolete) A miscarriage; an untimely birth; an abortion. [Attested from around (1350 to 1470) until the mid 17th century.]
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese 墮胎 and include miscarriage sense 流產.

example.english: In older usage, abort could mean a miscarriage.
example.chinese: 在較舊用法中，abort 可指流產或墮胎。

corrections.english: (transitive, computing) To terminate a process prior to completion.
corrections.reason: The computing verb means to terminate a process, not miscarry.

example.english: Press escape to abort the process.
example.chinese: 按 Escape 鍵可中止這個程序。

corrections.english: (transitive, biology) To cause an organism to develop minimally; to cause rudimentary development to happen; to prevent maturation. [First attested in the mid 19th century.]
corrections.reason: The biology sense is preventing maturation or causing rudimentary development, not pregnancy miscarriage.

example.english: The mutation may abort normal development.
example.chinese: 這種突變可能使正常發育不全，並阻止成熟。

corrections.new_chinese: 早產兒；流產胎兒；未成熟事物
corrections.english: (obsolete) Someone or something born or brought forth prematurely; an abortion. [Attested from around (1150 to 1350) until the mid 18th century.]
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese and reflect obsolete noun sense of something born prematurely.

example.english: The obsolete noun abortive referred to something born prematurely.
example.chinese: 這個過時名詞 abortive 指早產兒或未成熟事物。

corrections.english: Failure to be present where one is expected, wanted, or needed; nonattendance; deficiency. [First attested around 1350 to 1470.]
corrections.reason: Absence is the state/fact of not being present; 請假 is requesting leave.

example.english: Her absence from the meeting was noticed.
example.chinese: 她缺席會議一事引起了注意。

corrections.english: That which is absurd; an absurd action; a logical contradiction. [First attested in the late 15th century.]
corrections.reason: Use Traditional Chinese and cover absurd action/contradiction, not only fallacy.

example.english: The absurdity of the rule was obvious.
example.chinese: 這條規則的荒謬顯而易見。

corrections.english: The quality of being absurd or inconsistent with obvious truth, reason, or sound judgment. [First attested in the early 16th century.]
corrections.reason: Absurdity here is the quality of being absurd; 谬論 is Simplified and too narrow.

example.english: They laughed at the absurdity of the claim.
example.chinese: 他們嘲笑那個說法的荒謬。

corrections.english: A large quantity; many. [First attested around 1150 to 1350.]
corrections.reason: Abundance means a large quantity; 複數 means plural.

example.english: There was an abundance of food at the festival.
example.chinese: 節日裡有大量食物。

corrections.english: (rare, pluralonly) The butting or boundary of land, particularly at the end; buttals. [First attested in the early 17th century.]
corrections.reason: Abuttal refers to the boundary or end of a parcel of land, not necessarily a national border.

example.english: The deed described the abuttal of the land.
example.chinese: 契約描述了該土地的鄰接邊界。

corrections.english: The frequency with which one has been present for a regular activity or set of events.
corrections.reason: Attendance here means frequency of being present, not simply appearance.

example.english: Her attendance improved this term.
example.chinese: 她這學期的出席率提高了。

corrections.english: The known (thing) (used in indicating something or someone just mentioned).
corrections.reason: The known thing just mentioned; convert Simplified 这 to Traditional 這.

example.english: This is never going to end.
example.chinese: 這件事永遠不會結束。

corrections.english: A foodstuff made by baking dough made from cereals.
corrections.reason: Bread is food made by baking dough; 面包 is Simplified, 麵包 is Traditional.

example.english: She bought a loaf of bread.
example.chinese: 她買了一條麵包。

corrections.english: (transitive) To receive (property, a title, etc.), by legal succession or bequest after the previous owner's death.
corrections.reason: Inherit means to receive property or a title by succession; convert Simplified 继承 to Traditional 繼承.

example.english: She will inherit the house from her aunt.
example.chinese: 她將從姑母那裡繼承這所房子。

corrections.english: In, at or to the place (that) or a place (that).
corrections.reason: Where asks or refers to a place; 哪儿 is Simplified, use Traditional 哪兒/哪裡.

example.english: Where are you going?
example.chinese: 你要去哪裡？

corrections.english: A circular device capable of rotating on its axis, facilitating movement or transportation or performing labour in machines.
corrections.reason: Wheel is a circular device that rotates; 轮子 is Simplified, 輪子 is Traditional.

example.english: The front wheel is broken.
example.chinese: 前輪子壞了。

corrections.english: A soft, fatty foodstuff made by churning the cream of milk (generally cow's milk).
corrections.reason: Butter is a dairy fat; 牛油 is common Traditional Chinese usage and 黃油 is also understood.

example.english: Spread some butter on the bread.
example.chinese: 在麵包上塗些牛油。

corrections.english: (transitive) To free from dirt; to clean, to purify.
corrections.reason: Cleanse means to free from dirt or purify; add 淨化 to cover purify sense.

example.english: Use clean water to cleanse the wound.
example.chinese: 用清水清洗傷口。

corrections.english: (transitive, or, intransitive) To look at and interpret letters or other information that is written.
corrections.reason: Read means to look at and interpret written information; current term is correct but add reviewed example.

example.english: She can read French.
example.chinese: 她能閱讀法文。

corrections.english: The early part of the day, especially from midnight to noon. [from 13th c.]
corrections.reason: Morning is the early part of the day; current term is correct but expand common Traditional variants.

example.english: I drink coffee every morning.
example.chinese: 我每天早上喝咖啡。

corrections.english: (architecture) A projecting part of a rampart or other fortification.
corrections.reason: Remove duplicate Simplified/Traditional forms; bastion is a projecting fortified part or stronghold.

example.english: The old bastion guarded the harbour.
example.chinese: 那座古老堡壘守衛著港口。

corrections.english: A wax secreted by bees from which they make honeycomb; or, the processed form of this wax used in the manufacture of various goods.
corrections.reason: Remove Simplified 蜡 and keep Traditional 蜂蠟.

example.english: Beeswax is used to make candles.
example.chinese: 蜂蠟可用來製作蠟燭。

corrections.old_chinese: 晴天霹雳 晴天霹靂
corrections.english: (informal, idiomatic) A complete surprise; something totally unexpected.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 霹靂 and remove duplicate Simplified form.

example.english: The news came as a bolt from the blue.
example.chinese: 這個消息如晴天霹靂。

corrections.english: A defensive wall or rampart.
corrections.reason: Bulwark is a defensive wall or rampart; remove duplicate Simplified form and clarify.

example.english: The city built a bulwark against attack.
example.chinese: 該城建造堡壘和防壁以抵禦攻擊。

corrections.english: A wild canine animal (Speothos venaticus), found in Central and South America.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 藪犬 and remove Simplified duplicate.

example.english: The bush dog lives in Central and South America.
example.chinese: 藪犬生活在中南美洲。

corrections.old_chinese: 尸身 屍身 尸體 屍體 尸首 屍首
corrections.english: The body of a dead animal, especially a vertebrate or other animal having flesh.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 屍 and remove duplicate Simplified variants.

example.english: The carcass was found near the road.
example.chinese: 屍體在路邊被發現。

corrections.english: A species of freshwater bream, Abramis brama.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 鯿 and remove mixed note.

example.english: The carp bream is a freshwater fish.
example.chinese: 歐鯿是一種淡水魚。

corrections.english: The act of judicially condemning, or adjudging guilty, unfit for use, or forfeited; the act of dooming to punishment or forfeiture.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 決 and include condemnation sense of judgment/condemnation.

example.english: The court issued a condemnation of the unsafe building.
example.chinese: 法院發出針對危險建築的判決書。

corrections.english: All those activities undertaken to neutralize or exploit the espionage activities of another nation or an enemy.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 諜 and clarify counterespionage as activity.

example.english: Counterespionage protects secrets from spies.
example.chinese: 反間諜活動保護機密免受間諜竊取。

corrections.english: A transliteration of the Ukrainian male given name Дмитро (Dmytro)
corrections.reason: Dmytro is a Ukrainian given name; current form has stray invisible character and less accurate transliteration.

example.english: Dmytro is a common Ukrainian given name.
example.chinese: 德米特羅是一個常見的烏克蘭男性名字。

corrections.old_chinese: 复線鐵路 tiě lù 複綫鐵路 鉄路
corrections.english: (rail) A section of railway line or tramline consisting of two parallel tracks, one being used in one direction and the other in the opposite direction.
corrections.reason: Normalize mixed Simplified/Traditional and remove pinyin fragments.

example.english: A double track allows trains to run in both directions.
example.chinese: 雙線鐵路可讓列車雙向行駛。

corrections.english: (US) An item of bedroom furniture, like a low chest of drawers (bureau), often with a mirror.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 屜 and remove duplicate mixed form.

example.english: She put her clothes in the dresser.
example.chinese: 她把衣服放進多屜櫃。

corrections.old_chinese: 雙頭壟斷 雙頭垄斷
corrections.english: (economics) An economic condition in which two sellers exert most control over the market of a commodity.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 壟 and remove Simplified duplicate.

example.english: A duopoly occurs when two sellers dominate a market.
example.chinese: 雙頭壟斷是指兩個賣方主導市場。

corrections.english: An inscription on a gravestone in memory of the deceased.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 誌 and 銘.

example.english: The epitaph was carved on the stone.
example.chinese: 墓誌銘刻在石碑上。

corrections.english: A well-known black-and-white corvid species, Pica pica, of Eurasian distribution.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 鵲 and remove Simplified duplicate.

example.english: The Eurasian magpie has black and white feathers.
example.chinese: 喜鵲有黑白相間的羽毛。

corrections.english: (baseball) The infield defensive player that stands near first base.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 壘 and remove slash duplicate.

example.english: The first baseman caught the throw.
example.chinese: 一壘手接住了傳球。

corrections.english: The process of dehydration by sublimation for preservation or convenience; the removal of moisture from a frozen material using vacuum.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 凍 and clarify freeze-drying.

example.english: Freeze-drying preserves the sample.
example.chinese: 冷凍乾燥可保存樣本。

corrections.english: (geology) An elongated block of the Earth's crust, bounded by faults, that has dropped relative to the surrounding area.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 塹 and remove duplicate Simplified form.

example.english: A graben forms when a block of crust drops.
example.chinese: 地塹是在地殼塊體下陷時形成的。

corrections.english: The act of hanging a person (or oneself) by the neck in order to kill that person (or to commit suicide).
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 絞 and remove Simplified duplicate.

example.english: Hanging was once used as a punishment.
example.chinese: 絞刑曾被用作刑罰。

corrections.old_chinese: 一杆進洞 一桿進洞
corrections.english: (golf) A hole that is completed by sinking the ball in a single shot or attempt, with one hit.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 桿 and normalize golf term.

example.english: She scored a hole in one.
example.chinese: 她打出了一桿進洞。

corrections.english: (pesäpallo) A three-base hit, a legal hit where the batter gets to third base without stopping and without the opposing team attempting to put the batter out on earlier bases, scoring one run immediately.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 壘 and remove duplicate Simplified form.

example.english: He hit a home run.
example.chinese: 他擊出全壘打。

corrections.old_chinese: 雜乱無章 雜亂無章
corrections.english: Contrary to logic; lacking sense or sound reasoning.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 亂 and clarify illogical as contrary to logic.

example.english: His argument was illogical.
example.chinese: 他的論點不合邏輯，雜亂無章。

corrections.english: A fried, filled pancake-like food eaten in Korea
corrections.reason: Current 約翰 is a name and mismatches Korean food jeon.

example.english: Jeon is a Korean fried pancake-like food.
example.chinese: 煎是一種韓式煎餅類食物。

corrections.english: A region, peninsula, and urban area of Hong Kong.
corrections.reason: Kowloon is a Hong Kong region; 庫侖/庫仑 is unrelated.

example.english: Kowloon is an urban area of Hong Kong.
example.chinese: 九龍是香港的一個市區。

corrections.reason: Use Traditional 凍 and clarify lyophilization.

example.english: Lyophilization removes water from frozen material.
example.chinese: 冷凍乾燥會從冷凍材料中移除水分。

corrections.english: Any of various species of small parrot primarily of tropical regions. [from 16th c.]
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 鸚鵡 and remove slash duplicate.

example.english: A parakeet is a small parrot.
example.chinese: 長尾小鸚鵡是一種小型鸚鵡。

corrections.old_chinese: 勸善懲惡 劝善懲惡
corrections.english: (by extension, generally) The fact of someone experiencing what they deserve for their actions, especially when this happens in an ironic manner.
corrections.reason: Poetic justice means deserved ironic outcome; normalize duplicate Simplified/Traditional phrase.

example.english: His defeat felt like poetic justice.
example.chinese: 他的失敗像是善惡有報的詩意正義。

corrections.english: Person who is not a government official who is hired to provide information about a person or group that is not widely available
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 偵探 and clarify private detective.

example.english: The family hired a private detective.
example.chinese: 那家人聘請了一名私家偵探。

corrections.english: A religious custom.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 儀式 and remove Simplified duplicate.

example.english: The rite is performed every spring.
example.chinese: 這項儀式每年春天舉行。

corrections.english: (by extension) A prescribed behavior.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 儀式 and include extended prescribed behavior sense.

example.english: The handshake became a rite of welcome.
example.chinese: 握手成了歡迎的儀式和慣例。

corrections.word: rome wasn't built in a day
corrections.old_chinese: 冰凍三尺，非一日之寒 冰冻三尺，非一日之寒
corrections.english: (idiomatic) It takes a long time to create something complicated or impressive.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 凍 and remove duplicate Simplified form.

example.english: Rome was not built in a day, so be patient.
example.chinese: 冰凍三尺，非一日之寒，所以要有耐心。

corrections.english: (baseball) The infield defensive player that stands between the first baseman and the shortstop, normally on the right field side of second base.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 壘 and remove slash duplicate.

example.english: The second baseman threw to first.
example.chinese: 二壘手把球傳向一壘。

corrections.english: (now, _, rare) Added above what is needed or necessary; superfluous, redundant.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 餘 and remove pinyin annotation.

example.english: The extra note was supervacaneous.
example.chinese: 那條額外註記是多餘的。

corrections.english: (baseball) The infield defensive player that stands near third base.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 壘 and remove slash duplicate.

example.english: The third baseman fielded the ground ball.
example.chinese: 三壘手接住了滾地球。

corrections.old_chinese: 弗里西亞語 弗里西語
corrections.english: A language spoken mostly in the province of Friesland, in the north of the Netherlands; one of the Frisian languages (or language varieties).
corrections.reason: West Frisian is a Frisian language; current generic terms are less precise.

example.english: West Frisian is spoken in Friesland.
example.chinese: 西弗里斯蘭語在弗里斯蘭使用。

corrections.english: To grapple or otherwise contend with an opponent in order to throw or force them to the ground, chiefly as a sport or in unarmed combat.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 鬥 and clarify wrestle as grapple/contend.

example.english: The athletes wrestle on the mat.
example.chinese: 運動員在墊上摔跤搏鬥。

corrections.old_chinese: 烏魯木齊 乌魯木齊
corrections.english: A prefecture-level city, the capital of the Xinjiang autonomous region, China.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 烏 and remove duplicate Simplified form.

example.english: Ürümqi is the capital of Xinjiang.
example.chinese: 烏魯木齊是新疆的首府。

corrections.english: (transitive, crime) To incite; to assist or encourage by aid or countenance in crime. [ca. 1350–1470]
corrections.reason: Abet means to incite or assist in a crime; 激励 is Simplified and too positive.

example.english: He was charged with abetting the robbery.
example.chinese: 他被控教唆並幫助犯罪。

corrections.english: In an abject fashion; with great shame; desperately. [first attested c. 1350–1470]
corrections.reason: Abjectly means in an abject, shameful, or desperate manner; 沮丧地 is Simplified and incomplete.

example.english: He apologized abjectly.
example.chinese: 他卑屈地道歉。

corrections.english: Mentioned or named before; aforesaid.
corrections.reason: Above-mentioned means mentioned before, not merely 上面.

example.english: The above-mentioned rule still applies.
example.chinese: 上述的規則仍然適用。

corrections.english: (dated) Without a certain confine; outside the house; away from one's abode. [from 1150–1350)]
corrections.reason: Abroad can mean outside the home or in/to a foreign country; 外面 is too vague.

example.english: She studied abroad for a year.
example.chinese: 她在國外讀了一年書。

corrections.english: The capital city of the United Arab Emirates; the capital of Abu Dhabi emirate.
corrections.reason: Abu Dhabi is conventionally rendered 阿布達比 in Traditional Chinese.

example.english: Abu Dhabi is the capital of the United Arab Emirates.
example.chinese: 阿布達比是阿拉伯聯合大公國的首都。

corrections.english: In an abundant manner; in a sufficient degree; in large measure. [First attested around 1350 to 1470.]
corrections.reason: Abundantly means in large measure or sufficiently; convert Simplified 丰 to Traditional 豐.

example.english: The garden was abundantly supplied with water.
example.chinese: 花園有豐富地供水。

corrections.english: (manner) Agreeably; correspondingly; suitably
corrections.reason: Accordingly can mean correspondingly or as a result; 合适 is Simplified and too narrow.

example.english: The rules changed, and we acted accordingly.
example.chinese: 規則改變了，我們也相應地行動。

corrections.english: (neurotransmitter) A neurotransmitter in humans and other animals. It is an ester of acetic acid and choline with chemical formula CH3COOCH2CH2N+(CH3)3.
corrections.reason: Acetylcholine is the neurotransmitter 乙醯膽鹼; current term is Simplified.

example.english: Acetylcholine acts as a neurotransmitter.
example.chinese: 乙醯膽鹼是一種神經傳遞物質。

corrections.english: (organic compound) An organic acid involved in the isomerization of citrate to isocitrate in the citric acid cycle.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 烏頭酸 for aconitic acid.

example.english: Aconitic acid occurs in the citric acid cycle.
example.chinese: 烏頭酸出現在檸檬酸循環中。

corrections.english: A port city in northern Israel, holiest city in the Baháʼí Faith.
corrections.reason: Acre city in northern Israel is 阿卡, not the Brazilian state 阿克里州.

example.english: Acre is a port city in northern Israel.
example.chinese: 阿卡是以色列北部的港口城市。

corrections.english: Any of various similar units of area in other systems.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 畝 for acre as an area unit.

example.english: The farm covers one acre.
example.chinese: 這座農場面積為一英畝。

corrections.english: An opinion offered to guide behavior in an effort to be helpful.
corrections.reason: Advice means guidance or recommendation; convert Simplified 劝 to Traditional 勸.

example.english: She gave me useful advice.
example.chinese: 她給了我有用的勸告。

corrections.english: A classic epic poem, written in Latin by Virgil in the 1st century BCE (between 29 and 19 BCE), that tells the legendary story of Aeneas fleeing Troy and settling in Italy as ancestor of the Romans.
corrections.reason: Aeneid is an epic poem; convert Simplified 纪 to Traditional 紀.

example.english: The Aeneid was written by Virgil.
example.chinese: 《埃涅阿斯紀》由維吉爾創作。

corrections.english: (seismology) An earthquake that follows in the same vicinity as another, usually larger, earthquake (the "mainshock").
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 餘震 for aftershock.

example.english: An aftershock followed the main earthquake.
example.chinese: 主震之後發生了一次餘震。

corrections.english: (historical period) A period of European intellectual history during which the Enlightenment, an intellectual movement emphasizing the importance of logic and reason, flourished, from part of the 17th century through much of the 18th century.
corrections.reason: Age of Enlightenment is 啟蒙時代; convert Simplified 启.

example.english: The Age of Enlightenment emphasized reason.
example.chinese: 啟蒙時代強調理性。

corrections.english: Characterized by aggression; highly combative; prone to behave in a way that involves attacking (especially if unjustly) or arguing.
corrections.reason: Aggressive means combative or prone to attack; convert 斗 to 鬥 and clarify.

example.english: The dog became aggressive.
example.chinese: 那隻狗變得好鬥的。

corrections.english: (pathology, of a tumour or disease) That spreads quickly or extensively; virulent; malignant.
corrections.reason: For tumour or disease, aggressive means spreading quickly or malignant, not 好斗的.

example.english: The doctor diagnosed an aggressive tumour.
example.chinese: 醫生診斷出侵襲性的腫瘤。

corrections.english: In an aggressive manner.
corrections.reason: Aggressively means in an aggressive manner; convert 斗 to 鬥.

example.english: He argued aggressively.
example.chinese: 他好鬥地爭辯。

corrections.english: A tip, originally of metal and often decorative, on a ribbon or cord that makes lacing two parts of a garment or garments together easier, as in corset lacings, "points" (lacing hose or trousers to jacket or doublet) or sleeves to a bodice.
corrections.reason: Aiglet is the tip on a lace or cord, not 生面團.

example.english: The aiglet kept the lace from fraying.
example.chinese: 金屬包頭防止鞋帶磨散。

corrections.english: (transitive) To direct or point (e.g. a weapon), at a particular object; to direct, as a missile, an act, or a proceeding, at, to, or against an object
corrections.reason: Aim means direct or point at a target; convert 准 to 準.

example.english: She aimed the arrow at the target.
example.chinese: 她把箭瞄準目標。

corrections.english: (chemistry) One of a class of caustic bases, such as soda, soda ash, caustic soda, potash, ammonia, and lithia, whose distinguishing characteristics are dissolving in alcohol and water, uniting with oils and fats to form soap, neutralizing and forming salts with acids, turning to brown several vegetable yellows, and changing reddened litmus to blue.
corrections.reason: Alkali is a caustic base or alkaline substance; use Traditional 鹼 and avoid a one-character learner definition.

example.english: An alkali can neutralize an acid.
example.chinese: 鹼性物質可以中和酸。

corrections.english: (chemistry) The state of being, or the degree to which a thing is, alkaline.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 鹼度 for alkalinity.

example.english: The alkalinity of the water was measured.
example.chinese: 水的鹼度已被測量。

corrections.english: (in the plural) The resins of the tree Aquilaria malaccensis (syn. Aquilaria agallocha), known for their fragrant aroma, produced after infection by the fungus Phialophora parasitica.
corrections.reason: This plural aloe sense refers to resins from Aquilaria, not aloe plant 蘆薈.

example.english: Aloes were valued for their fragrance.
example.chinese: 沉香脂因其香氣而受到重視。

corrections.english: Any plant of the large and variable genus Aloe.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 薈 for aloe plant.

example.english: Aloe is often grown as a houseplant.
example.chinese: 蘆薈常被種作室內植物。

corrections.english: With a loud voice, or great noise; loudly; audibly.
corrections.reason: Aloud means with a loud or audible voice, not 厉害.

example.english: Please read the sentence aloud.
example.chinese: 請出聲地讀出這個句子。

corrections.english: An angry or heated dispute.
corrections.reason: Altercation means a heated dispute; convert 争 to 爭.

example.english: The altercation happened outside the shop.
example.chinese: 爭吵發生在商店外。

corrections.english: Angry or heated disputation.
corrections.reason: Altercation means angry disputation; convert 争 to 爭.

example.english: Their altercation lasted several minutes.
example.chinese: 他們的激烈爭論持續了幾分鐘。

corrections.english: The area or sphere of control and influence of something.
corrections.reason: Ambit means area or sphere of control and influence, not 球面.

example.english: The issue lies within the ambit of the law.
example.chinese: 這個問題屬於法律的範圍。

corrections.english: An unhurried leisurely walk or stroll.
corrections.reason: Amble is an unhurried walk, not 對側步.

example.english: We went for an amble by the river.
example.chinese: 我們沿河悠閒散步。

corrections.english: Compensation for an injury or loss; recompense; reparation.
corrections.reason: Amends means compensation or reparation; convert 补偿 to Traditional 補償 and include 賠償.

example.english: He tried to make amends for his mistake.
example.chinese: 他試圖為自己的錯誤作出補償。

corrections.word: and a partridge in a pear tree
corrections.english: (humorous) Used at the end of a list of items to emphasize its length. [from 20th c.]
corrections.reason: This humorous phrase is used to end a long list, not 云雀.

example.english: She listed gifts, chores, errands, and a partridge in a pear tree.
example.chinese: 她列出禮物、家務、差事等等，還有一大堆。

corrections.english: (pharmaceutical effect) Destructive to parasitic worms.
corrections.reason: Anthelmintic adjective means destructive to parasitic worms; convert 虫 to 蟲.

example.english: The drug has an anthelmintic effect.
example.chinese: 這種藥有驅蟲的效果。

corrections.english: (medicine) A drug for the treatment of worm infestation, either by killing the worms or by causing them to be expelled from the body.
corrections.reason: Anthelmintic noun means a drug for worm infestation; convert 虫 to 蟲 and clarify.

example.english: The doctor prescribed an anthelmintic.
example.chinese: 醫生開了一種驅蟲藥。

corrections.english: The largest city and provincial capital of the province of Antwerp, Belgium.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 衛 for Antwerp.

example.english: Antwerp is a city in Belgium.
example.chinese: 安特衛普是比利時的一座城市。

corrections.english: (countable, biblical) The written account of a revelation of hidden things given by God to a chosen prophet.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 啟示錄.

example.english: The Apocalypse describes a revelation.
example.chinese: 《啟示錄》描述一場神啟。

corrections.english: (Christianity) Revelation (last book of the Bible, composed of twenty-two chapters, which narrates a vision of the end times).
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 啟示錄 for the biblical book Revelation.

example.english: The Apocalypse is the last book of the New Testament.
example.chinese: 《啟示錄》是新約聖經的最後一卷。

corrections.english: Suitable for application, relevant.
corrections.reason: Applicable means relevant or suitable for application; convert 适 to 適.

example.english: This rule is applicable to all members.
example.chinese: 這是一條適用的規則，適用於所有成員。

corrections.english: Anticipating something with anxiety, fear, or doubt; reluctant.
corrections.reason: Apprehensive means anxious or fearful, not merely 心神不宁 in Simplified.

example.english: She felt apprehensive before the exam.
example.chinese: 她考試前感到憂慮的和不安。

corrections.english: In an appropriate manner; properly; suitably.
corrections.reason: Appropriately means in a suitable manner; convert 适 to 適.

example.english: Please dress appropriately for the event.
example.chinese: 請為活動適當地穿著。

corrections.english: Timely; at a good time.
corrections.reason: Apropos as adverb means timely or at a good time; convert 适 to 適.

example.english: His comment came apropos.
example.chinese: 他的評論來得適時地。

corrections.english: Suitable; appropriate; fit or fitted; suited.
corrections.reason: Apt means suitable or appropriate; convert 适 to 適 and make adjective explicit.

example.english: That is an apt description.
example.chinese: 那是一個合適的描述。

corrections.english: In an apt or suitable manner; fittingly; appropriately; suitably
corrections.reason: Aptly means suitably or fittingly; convert 适 to 適.

example.english: The book is aptly titled.
example.chinese: 這本書的標題起得很合適地。

corrections.english: (trigonometry) Any of several single-valued or multivalued functions that are inverses of the cosine function.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 餘 for arccosine.

example.english: Arccosine is the inverse of cosine.
example.chinese: 反餘弦是餘弦的反函數。

corrections.english: A professional who designs buildings or other structures, or who prepares plans and superintends construction.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 建築師 for architect.

example.english: The architect designed the museum.
example.chinese: 建築師設計了那座博物館。

corrections.english: A person who plans, devises or contrives the achievement of a desired result.
corrections.reason: Architect figuratively means one who plans or devises, not necessarily 建筑師.

example.english: She was the architect of the reform.
example.chinese: 她是這場改革的策劃者。

corrections.english: The art and science of designing and managing the construction of buildings and other structures, particularly if they are well proportioned and decorated.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 建築學 for architecture.

example.english: He studied architecture in college.
example.chinese: 他在大學修讀建築學。

corrections.english: (astronomy) The brightest moon of the planet Uranus.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 衛 for Ariel, a moon of Uranus.

example.english: Ariel is a moon of Uranus.
example.chinese: 天衛一是天王星的一顆衛星。

corrections.english: (Greek god) The Greek goddess of the hunt, wild animals, and wilderness; the daughter of Leto and Zeus; the sister of Apollo. (Compare Diana.)
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 彌 for Artemis.

example.english: Artemis is a Greek goddess.
example.chinese: 阿耳忒彌斯是希臘女神。

corrections.english: As is appropriate to the circumstances; particularly where a choice must be made between two options.
corrections.reason: As the case may be means as appropriate to circumstances, not 若時機合适.

example.english: The rule applies to parents or guardians, as the case may be.
example.chinese: 這條規則適用於父母或監護人，視情況而定。

corrections.english: (Christianity) The Wednesday 46 days before Easter in a given year, (Catholicism) traditionally observed as the beginning of Lent and a day of strict abstention from certain foods and pleasures.
corrections.reason: Ash Wednesday is the first day of Lent in Western Christianity; convert 斋 to 齋 and clarify.

example.english: Ash Wednesday marks the beginning of Lent.
example.chinese: 聖灰星期三標誌著大齋期開始。

corrections.english: (medicine) Suffering from astigmatism.
corrections.reason: Astigmatic means suffering from astigmatism; 亂視 is Japanese/Simplified-mixed style.

example.english: The patient is astigmatic.
example.chinese: 這名病人是散光的。

corrections.english: (analysis) A straight line which a curve approaches arbitrarily closely as it goes to infinity. The limit of the curve; its tangent "at infinity".
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 漸 for asymptote.

example.english: The curve approaches the asymptote.
example.chinese: 曲線逐漸接近漸近線。

corrections.english: (Greek god) The son of Iapetus and Clymene, war leader of the Titans ordered by the god Zeus to support the sky on his shoulders; father to the Hesperides, the Hyades, and the Pleiades; king of the legendary Atlantis.
corrections.reason: Atlas Greek god is 阿特拉斯, not Saturn moon 土衛十五.

example.english: Atlas was ordered to hold up the sky.
example.chinese: 阿特拉斯被命令支撐天空。

corrections.english: (astronomy) A crater in the last quadrant of the moon.
corrections.reason: This Atlas sense is a lunar crater, not Saturn moon 土衛十五.

example.english: Atlas is a crater on the Moon.
example.chinese: 阿特拉斯環形山位於月球上。

corrections.english: (rocketry, military, US) An SM-65, an early ICBM, soon developed into a long-lived orbital launch vehicle series.
corrections.reason: This Atlas sense is a rocket/missile series, not Saturn moon 土衛十五.

example.english: The Atlas rocket launched the payload.
example.chinese: 阿特拉斯火箭發射了載荷。

corrections.english: (grammar) A word that qualifies a noun.
corrections.reason: In grammar, attribute means a word that qualifies a noun, not 决定性的.

example.english: In this phrase, the adjective functions as an attribute.
example.chinese: 在這個片語中，形容詞作定語。

corrections.english: (mineral) A yellow mineral with tetragonal crystals, Ca(UO2)2(PO4)2·10-12H2O.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 鈾 and 雲 for autunite.

example.english: Autunite is a uranium mineral.
example.chinese: 鈣鈾雲母是一種含鈾礦物。

corrections.english: One who avenges or vindicates.
corrections.reason: Use Traditional 復 for avenger.

example.english: The avenger sought justice.
example.chinese: 復仇者尋求正義。

corrections.english: (transitive) To accept (a decision or law) and act in accordance with it; to conform to (a decision or law); to acquiesce to (a decision or law).
corrections.reason: Abide by means to accept and act according to a law, decision, or rule; 提交 is unrelated.

example.english: Everyone must abide by the rules.
example.chinese: 每個人都必須遵守規則。

corrections.english: Of a person: cast down in hope or spirit; showing utter helplessness, hopelessness, or resignation; also, grovelling; ingratiating; servile. [from mid 14th c.]
corrections.reason: Abject in this sense means cast down, hopeless, or servile, not morally despicable 卑鄙.

example.english: They lived in abject poverty.
example.chinese: 他們生活在卑微的絕望貧困中。

corrections.english: (intransitive) To hide, to be in hiding or concealment.
corrections.reason: Abscond here means to hide or be in hiding; 掩藏 is awkward and incomplete.

example.english: The suspect absconded after the trial.
example.chinese: 嫌疑人在審訊後潛逃並躲藏。

corrections.english: (figurative) Extremely bad; terrible.
corrections.reason: Abysmal figuratively means extremely bad, not underground.

example.english: The team gave an abysmal performance.
example.chinese: 那支隊伍的表現是糟透的。

corrections.english: (obsolete) The knowledge disseminated in an Academy. [Attested from the early 17th century until the mid 18th century.]
corrections.reason: This obsolete academy sense means knowledge disseminated in an academy, not a seminary.

example.english: The text reflects the academy of its time.
example.chinese: 這篇文本反映了當時的學院知識。

corrections.english: The state of being accepted.
corrections.reason: Acceptance means the state of being accepted, not assimilation.

example.english: Her acceptance into the program was confirmed.
example.chinese: 她被該計劃接受一事已獲確認。

corrections.english: (law) An agreeing to the action, proposals, or terms of another by some act which results in the conclusion of a legally binding contract; the reception or taking of a thing bought as that for which it was bought, or as that agreed to be delivered, or the taking of possession of a thing as owner.
corrections.reason: Legal acceptance means agreeing to terms in a binding contract, not generic agreement only.

example.english: Acceptance of the offer created a contract.
example.chinese: 接受條款和承諾使合約成立。

corrections.english: (rare) A midwife.
corrections.reason: Accoucheuse is a midwife, not grandmother.

example.english: The accoucheuse helped deliver the baby.
example.chinese: 助產士幫忙接生嬰兒。

corrections.english: One who accuses; one who brings a charge of crime or fault.
corrections.reason: Accuser is one who accuses; 原告 alone is too narrow and may be civil plaintiff.

example.english: The accuser gave evidence in court.
example.chinese: 控告者在法庭上作證。

corrections.new_chinese: 秘密王牌；出奇制勝的一招
corrections.english: (idiom) A surprise advantage of which others are not aware.
corrections.reason: Ace up one’s sleeve is a hidden advantage, not simply joker.

example.english: Her experience was an ace up her sleeve.
example.chinese: 她的經驗是她秘密王牌。

corrections.english: Tasting sour or bitter.
corrections.reason: Acerbic can mean sour/bitter tasting or sharp in tone, not rough.

example.english: The fruit had an acerbic taste.
example.chinese: 這種水果有酸澀的味道。

corrections.english: (video games) An award for completing a particular task or meeting an objective in a video game.
corrections.reason: In video games, achievement is an award for completing a task; 成功 is too broad.

example.english: He unlocked a rare achievement in the game.
example.chinese: 他在遊戲中解鎖了一項罕見成就。

corrections.english: A person or persons with whom one is acquainted. [from 14th c.]
corrections.reason: Acquaintance is a person one knows, not necessarily a friend.

example.english: She is an acquaintance from work.
example.chinese: 她是一位工作上認識的熟人。

corrections.english: (legal) A legal decision that someone is not guilty with which they have been charged, or the formal dismissal of a charge by some other legal process. [from 15th c.]
corrections.reason: Acquittal is a legal decision that someone is not guilty, not pardon/赦免.

example.english: The jury returned an acquittal.
example.chinese: 陪審團作出無罪判決。

corrections.english: (figuratively) Angry, acid, and sharp in delivering argumentative replies: bitter, mean-spirited, sharp in language or tone. [from early 17th c.]
corrections.reason: Acrimonious means bitter or sharp in argument, not rough.

example.english: They had an acrimonious debate.
example.chinese: 他們進行了一場尖刻而激烈的辯論。

corrections.english: Existing in reality, not just potentially; really acted or acting; occurring in fact. [from 14th c.]
corrections.reason: Actual means existing in reality, not valid/effective.

example.english: The actual cost was higher than expected.
example.chinese: 實際的成本比預期高。

corrections.english: (intransitive) To make oneself comfortable to a new thing.
corrections.reason: Adapt intransitive means to adjust oneself to new conditions, not assimilate.

example.english: Children adapt quickly to new schools.
example.chinese: 孩子很快適應新學校。

corrections.english: (symptom) an abnormally enlarged mass of lymphatic tissue at the back of the pharynx, often obstructing the nasal and ear passages and hindering speaking and breathing in young children.
corrections.reason: Adenoid is enlarged lymphatic tissue near the pharynx, not tonsil.

example.english: The child had enlarged adenoids.
example.chinese: 那名兒童有腺樣體肥大。

corrections.english: One fully skilled or well versed in anything; a proficient
corrections.reason: Adept noun means a proficient person, not 大家 as a vague honorific.

example.english: She is an adept at negotiation.
example.chinese: 她是談判能手。

corrections.english: Adhesive, sticking to something.
corrections.reason: Adherent adjective means sticking to something, not sticker.

example.english: The adherent label was hard to remove.
example.chinese: 那張黏著的標籤很難撕掉。

corrections.english: (intransitive, formal, uncommon) To move as a group from one place to another.
corrections.reason: Adjourn can mean to move as a group from one place to another; 延緩 is not this sense.

example.english: After dinner, we adjourned to the lounge.
example.chinese: 晚餐後，我們移往別處，到休息室去。

corrections.english: A faint sketch; a brief representation, an outline.
corrections.reason: Adumbration is a faint sketch or outline, not foundation.

example.english: The essay offered an adumbration of the theory.
example.chinese: 這篇文章提出了該理論的概要。

corrections.english: (tennis) The score where one player wins a point after deuce but needs the next to carry the game.
corrections.reason: In tennis, advantage is the score after deuce, not benefit.

example.english: She won the next point and gained advantage.
example.chinese: 她贏得下一分，取得局末平分後領先。

corrections.english: (intransitive) To provide information about a person or goods and services to influence others. [from 18th c.]
corrections.reason: Advertise means to provide information to influence buyers or others, not publish.

example.english: The company will advertise the new product.
example.chinese: 公司將為新產品做廣告宣傳。

corrections.english: Mild; benign.
corrections.reason: Affable means mild and easy to talk to, not merely benevolent.

example.english: He was an affable host.
example.chinese: 他是一位和藹親切的主人。

corrections.english: (dated) Tributary.
corrections.reason: Affluent dated adjective means tributary, not distributary 分流.

example.english: An affluent stream joined the river.
example.chinese: 一條支流的溪流匯入河中。

corrections.english: (obsolete) A hostile encounter or meeting.
corrections.reason: Affront noun obsolete means hostile encounter or meeting, not duel specifically.

example.english: The old text described an affront between rivals.
example.chinese: 舊文本描述了敵對者之間的衝突。

corrections.english: Regretful, sorry; expressing a reluctance to face an unpleasant situation. [with that (+ clause) or clause; or with so or not]
corrections.reason: Afraid with that-clause can mean regretful or sorry, not 可惜 as an adjective.

example.english: I am afraid that I cannot help.
example.chinese: 我很抱歉的告訴你，我不能幫忙。

corrections.english: (relative to past) Having existed for so great a period of time that its longevity cannot be expressed.
corrections.reason: Ageless here means existing for an immeasurably long time; 永恒 should be Traditional 永恆 and adjectival.

example.english: The mountains seemed ageless.
example.chinese: 群山看似亙古的、長久存在的。

corrections.english: (intransitive) To make a stipulation by way of settling differences or determining a price; to exchange promises; to come to terms or to a common resolve; to promise.
corrections.reason: Agree here means to settle differences or come to terms, not 忖度.

example.english: They agreed on a price.
example.chinese: 他們達成協議，商定了價格。

corrections.new_chinese: 可喜之處；令人愉快的特質
corrections.english: (obsolete, chiefly, _, in the plural) An agreeable quality.
corrections.reason: Agreement obsolete plural means agreeable qualities, not coordination.

example.english: The poet praised the agreements of the scene.
example.chinese: 詩人讚美那景象的可喜之處。

corrections.english: (Christianity, chiefly, _, Anglicanism, Roman Catholicism) A long, white robe worn by priests and other ministers, underneath most of the other vestments.
corrections.reason: Alb is a long white robe worn by priests, not dawn.

example.english: The priest wore an alb during the service.
example.chinese: 神父在禮拜中穿著白長袍。

corrections.english: To convey or transfer to another, as title, property, or right; to part voluntarily with ownership of.
corrections.reason: Alienate in law means to transfer property or rights, not sell only.

example.english: The owner may alienate the property.
example.chinese: 業主可以轉讓該財產。

corrections.english: To cause one to feel unable to relate.
corrections.reason: Alienate means to make someone feel unable to relate, not steal.

example.english: His remarks alienated many supporters.
example.chinese: 他的言論使疏遠了許多支持者。

corrections.english: (legal, sometimes, pejorative) A court-mandated allowance made to a former spouse by a divorced or legally separated person.
corrections.reason: Alimony is court-mandated support paid to a former spouse, not guesthouse.

example.english: He pays alimony every month.
example.chinese: 他每月支付贍養費。

corrections.new_chinese: 神智正常的；頭腦清楚的
corrections.english: Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see all,‎ there.
corrections.reason: All there means mentally competent; 傻子 is the opposite/insulting noun.

example.english: After the accident, he was not quite all there.
example.chinese: 事故後，他不太像是神智正常的、頭腦清楚的。

corrections.english: (transitive) To make quiet or put at rest; to pacify or appease; to quell; to calm.
corrections.reason: Allay means to calm or pacify, not 和缓 as Simplified/awkward.

example.english: The medicine helped allay the pain.
example.chinese: 這種藥有助於減輕疼痛。

corrections.english: (engineering) A planned deviation between an exact dimension and a nominal or theoretical dimension.
corrections.reason: Allowance in engineering is planned deviation between exact and nominal dimensions, not game.

example.english: The design includes a small allowance.
example.chinese: 設計中包含小幅公差。

corrections.english: (religion) The response of the congregation speaking alternately with the minister.
corrections.reason: Alternation in religion means congregational responses alternating with minister, not answer alone.

example.english: The service included alternation between priest and congregation.
example.chinese: 禮拜包含神父與會眾的交替應答。

corrections.english: (geometry) The distance measured perpendicularly from a figure's vertex to the opposite side of the vertex.
corrections.reason: In geometry, altitude is the perpendicular distance from a vertex, not elevation above sea level.

example.english: Draw the altitude of the triangle.
example.chinese: 畫出三角形的高度線。

corrections.english: The post or office of ambassador.
corrections.reason: Ambassadorship means the post or office of ambassador, not embassy.

example.english: Her ambassadorship lasted four years.
example.chinese: 她的大使任期持續了四年。

corrections.english: The administration of anesthesia.
corrections.reason: Anesthetization is administration of anesthesia, not the study of anesthesia.

example.english: Anesthetization began before the operation.
example.chinese: 手術前開始施行麻醉。

corrections.english: Extreme pain, either of body or mind; excruciating distress.
corrections.reason: Anguish means extreme physical or mental pain, not cross.

example.english: She cried out in anguish.
example.chinese: 她因極度痛苦而喊叫。

corrections.english: Material, of an animal or most often vegetable origin, prepared (especially industrially) as feed for domestic or farm animals.
corrections.reason: Animal feed is material prepared as feed for animals, not ration/定量.

example.english: The farm stores animal feed in the barn.
example.chinese: 農場把動物飼料存放在穀倉裡。

corrections.english: (linguistics) conversion from the inanimate to animate grammatical category
corrections.reason: In linguistics, animation is conversion from inanimate to animate grammatical category, not cartoon.

example.english: The language shows animation of some nouns.
example.chinese: 該語言顯示某些名詞轉為有生性。

corrections.english: One who makes announcements.
corrections.reason: Announcer is one who makes announcements, not necessarily host.

example.english: The announcer read the news.
example.chinese: 播報員讀出新聞。

corrections.english: (literary, archaic) A feeling of discomfort or vexation caused by what one dislikes.
corrections.reason: Annoy noun means a feeling of discomfort or vexation, not boredom.

example.english: The delay caused great annoy.
example.chinese: 延誤造成很大的煩惱。

corrections.english: (botany) A structure surrounding a sporangium (or part of it) which shrinks and causes it to rupture for spore dispersal; specifically, in a fern: a structure around about two-thirds of the sporangium consisting of differentially thick-walled cells which dry and distort the sporangium; and in a moss: a complete ring of cells around the tip of the sporangium which dissolves to cause the tip to detach.
corrections.reason: Botanical annulus is a ring-like structure around a sporangium, not crown.

example.english: The fern has an annulus on the sporangium.
example.chinese: 蕨類孢子囊上有環帶。

corrections.english: (geometry) The region in a plane between two concentric circles of different radii.
corrections.reason: Geometric annulus is the region between concentric circles, not crown.

example.english: The shaded annulus lies between two circles.
example.chinese: 陰影的圓環是兩個同心圓之間的環形區域。

corrections.english: (topology) Any topological space homeomorphic to the region in a plane between two concentric circles of different radii.
corrections.reason: Topological annulus is a space homeomorphic to a ring-shaped region, not crown.

example.english: The surface is topologically an annulus.
example.chinese: 這個曲面在拓撲上是環形空間。

corrections.new_chinese: 連帽防風外套；派克大衣
corrections.english: A heavy weatherproof jacket with an attached hood; a parka or windcheater.
corrections.reason: Anorak is a heavy weatherproof jacket, not down jacket specifically.

example.english: She wore an anorak in the rain.
example.chinese: 她下雨時穿著連帽防風外套。

corrections.english: (physics) A region of maximum amplitude situated between adjacent nodes of a vibrating body, such as a string.
corrections.reason: Antinode is a region of maximum amplitude, not abdomen.

example.english: The string vibrates with an antinode at the center.
example.chinese: 弦在中央形成一個波腹。

corrections.english: (medicine) A medicine that counteracts venom, an antidote to biological poisons from venomous animals such as snakes and spiders.
corrections.reason: Antivenom counteracts venom, distinct from general antitoxin.

example.english: The hospital stocked antivenom for snakebites.
example.chinese: 醫院備有治療蛇咬的抗蛇毒血清。

corrections.english: A primate of the superfamily Hominoidea, generally larger than monkeys and distinguished from them by having no tail.
corrections.reason: Ape is a tailless hominoid primate, not monkey.

example.english: A gorilla is an ape, not a monkey.
example.chinese: 大猩猩是猿，不是猴子。

corrections.english: (derogatory) An uncivilized person.
corrections.reason: Derogatory ape means an uncivilized person, not monkey literally.

example.english: He used ape as an insult for a rude person.
example.chinese: 他用「猿」來辱罵粗野的人。

corrections.english: (by extension) Of dubious veracity; of questionable accuracy or truthfulness; anecdotal or in the nature of an urban legend.
corrections.reason: Apocryphal means dubious in truth or accuracy, not simply false words.

example.english: The story is probably apocryphal.
example.chinese: 這個故事很可能是真實性可疑的杜撰傳聞。

corrections.english: To make quiet; to calm; to reduce to a state of peace; to dispel (anger or hatred).
corrections.reason: Appease means to calm, pacify, or reduce anger; 和缓 is Simplified/awkward.

example.english: The apology helped appease the crowd.
example.chinese: 道歉有助於平息並安撫群眾。

corrections.english: To come to terms with; to adapt to the demands of.
corrections.reason: Appease can mean to come to terms with or adapt to demands, often by concession.

example.english: The leader tried to appease his opponents.
example.chinese: 領袖試圖姑息並遷就反對者。

corrections.english: (grammar) A construction in which one noun or noun phrase is placed with another as an explanatory equivalent, both of them having the same syntactic function in the sentence.
corrections.reason: Apposition in grammar is a noun phrase placed beside another as an explanatory equivalent; convert 并 to 並 and clarify.

example.english: In the phrase “my friend Alice,” Alice is in apposition.
example.chinese: 在「我的朋友 Alice」中，Alice 是同位語。

corrections.english: To anticipate (something, usually unpleasant); especially, to anticipate (something) with anxiety, dread, or fear; to dread, to fear.
corrections.reason: Apprehend here means to anticipate with anxiety or fear; 害怕 is close but incomplete.

example.english: They apprehended danger ahead.
example.chinese: 他們憂懼前方有危險。

corrections.english: (also, figuratively) An act of drawing near in place or time; an advancing or coming near.
corrections.reason: Approach noun in this sense is the act of drawing near, not method.

example.english: The approach of winter worried them.
example.chinese: 冬天的接近使他們擔心。

corrections.english: (also, figuratively) An avenue, passage, or way by which a building or place can be approached; an access.
corrections.reason: Approach as access means a way by which a place can be approached, not just entrance.

example.english: The north approach to the castle was blocked.
example.chinese: 通往城堡北面的通路被封鎖。
